title: croton catariae
intro: Croton catariae, also known as fever plant or king of the forest, is a tropical plant species native to Africa and Asia, characterized by its yellow sap and large leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects, which help to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Croton catariae include its ability to treat various conditions such as headaches, arthritis, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Croton catariae can be made by boiling the leaves or using the plant's sap to create a topical ointment or tincture for external use.
